steps.title: Identify the core mechanism of the universal pattern
steps.content:
The passage describes researchers believing many cultures use opposite linear directions for time—past on one side, future on the opposite side (example: Hebrew speakers).

Núñez claims this tendency is not universal, meaning not all cultures follow it.

Support for his claim would show Yupno speakers do not use opposite directional distinctions.

steps.title: Find what contradicts the proposed universal tendency
steps.content:
B shows that Yupno's future and past gestures do not point in opposite linear directions, directly contradicting the supposed universal pattern of opposite directional distinctions for time.

The other choices address unrelated factors: hand preference, grammatical patterns, and environmental context.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
